Introduction: The Goguryeo Relics and Historic Sites Tourism Area is located in Ji'an City, Jilin Province. It preserves the remains of the Goguryeo Kingdom, a slave-based state from the 1st to 7th centuries AD, including Gungnae City, Wandu Mountain City, royal tombs, and noble burial sites. Selling Points: UNESCO World Heritage Site; Oriental Pyramid — General's Tomb; Gwanggaeto Stele — Goguryeo's First Stele; The Most Complete Goguryeo Cultural Heritage Site Group in the World

Introduction: Yingxian Wooden Pagoda, officially known as the Sakyamuni Pagoda of Fogong Temple, was built in the second year of Qingning of the Liao Dynasty (1056 AD). It is the tallest and oldest existing wooden pagoda in China and is acclaimed as one of the "Three Great Pagodas of the World" alongside the Leaning Tower of Pisa in Italy and the Eiffel Tower in Paris. Standing at 67.31 meters, the pagoda contains not a single nail or rivet, constructed entirely through the interlocking of 54 types of dougong brackets and mortise-and-tenon joints. Despite enduring a millennium of wind, rain, and earthquakes, it remains standing firm.
Selling Points: One of the Three Great Pagodas of the World; China's tallest and oldest existing wooden pagoda; A miracle of thousand-year-old wooden architecture

Introduction: The Yalu River Broken Bridge is located on the banks of the Yalu River in Dandong City. It is the remaining part of the original Yalu River Bridge after it was destroyed by bombing, and serves as a historical witness to the War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea. Construction began in 1909, the bridge is 944.2 meters long, and it was bombed by U.S. aircraft in 1950, leaving only four arches intact on the Chinese side. Today, it is a national patriotic education base, where visitors can closely view the urban landscape of Sinuiju, North Korea.
Selling Points: Historical witness of the War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea; Patriotic education base; China-North Korea border landmark; Distant view of North Korea

Introduction: Hetu Ala City is located in Yongling Town, Xinbin Manchu Autonomous County. In 1616, Nurhaci ascended the throne here and proclaimed himself Khan, establishing the Later Jin regime, which is the birthplace of the Qing dynasty. The city preserves historical relics such as the Khan's Palace Main Hall, the White Banner Office, and the Khan King's Well.
Selling Points: First capital of Later Jin; Nurhaci's enthronement site; Birthplace of the Qing dynasty; Origin of Manchu culture

Introduction: Qing Yongling Mausoleum is the ancestral mausoleum of the Qing dynasty, located in Yongling Town, Xinbin Manchu Autonomous County. It is the ancestral tomb of the Aisin Gioro clan, the imperial family of the Qing emperors, and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The mausoleum houses the remains of Nurhaci's six-generation ancestor Mengge Tiemuer, great-grandfather Fuman, grandfather Awang'an, and father Takshi, among other Qing royal ancestors. Although the scale of Yongling Mausoleum is not large, its momentum is solemn and majestic, making it the foremost of the Three Tombs outside the Pass (Shengjing) of the Qing dynasty.

Introduction: Benxi Water Cave is located in the eastern mountainous area of Benxi City, 28 kilometers from the urban area, and is a national 5A-level tourist attraction. Introduction: Liaoyang White Pagoda is located in Bai Ta District, Liaoyang City, built during the Dading period of the Jin Dynasty (1161-1189 AD), standing 70.4 meters tall.
